evals.name: design-review-side-by-side
evals.prompt: I have 3 pairs of UI mockups under ./test-assets/ : ref-1.png / cur-1.png (Home), ref-2.png / cur-2.png (Settings), ref-3.png / cur-3.png (Profile). The 'ref' versions are the intended design; the 'cur' versions are what we actually built. Build me a single Excalidraw file that places each pair side by side on its own row, with a title per row, red circle annotations on specific differences, and a 'Key Differences' text block to the right of each pair. Each pair has a different layout (ref is stacked rows, cur is a tile grid) so please call that out specifically. Save the output as design-review.excalidraw in the current directory.
evals.expected_output: A valid .excalidraw JSON file with 3 rows, each containing 2 embedded images, at least one red ellipse per row, at least one text block per row, and a row title.

assertions.name: file_exists
assertions.check: output file exists at expected path

assertions.name: valid_json
assertions.check: file parses as JSON without errors

assertions.name: correct_top_level_type
assertions.check: top-level type field equals 'excalidraw'

assertions.name: has_required_top_level_keys
assertions.check: file contains 'elements', 'files', and 'appState' keys

assertions.name: embeds_all_six_images
assertions.check: files dict contains exactly 6 entries

assertions.name: six_image_elements_in_scene
assertions.check: elements array contains exactly 6 image elements

assertions.name: every_image_has_valid_file_id
assertions.check: every image element's fileId exists in files dict

assertions.name: at_least_three_red_ellipses
assertions.check: at least 3 red-stroke ellipses (one per row)

assertions.name: at_least_three_row_titles
assertions.check: at least 3 large text elements (row titles)

assertions.name: mentions_stacked_vs_tile_layout_difference
assertions.check: text mentions both 'row' and 'tile'

assertions.name: text_uses_helvetica_not_virgil
assertions.check: majority of text elements use fontFamily 2

assertions.name: all_required_element_fields_present
assertions.check: every element has all required schema fields

assertions.name: no_overlapping_text
assertions.check: no two text elements' bounding boxes materially overlap (layout-overlap failure mode)

assertions.name: text_does_not_overlap_images
assertions.check: no text element materially overlaps an image (short numbered markers <=3 chars excluded — they're intentional annotation keys)

assertions.name: annotation_ellipses_on_images
assertions.check: every red ellipse center lies within some image's bounds (annotations must point at actual content)

assertions.name: notes_right_of_images
assertions.check: each row's notes text block starts to the right of that row's images

evals.name: architecture-flowchart
evals.prompt: Generate an Excalidraw file showing a simple web application architecture. Components: Client (leftmost), API Gateway, three services (Auth, Users, Billing) in a vertical stack, and a Postgres database (rightmost, shared by Users and Billing). Use rectangles for services, arrows for data flow (Client → Gateway → each service; Users → Postgres; Billing → Postgres), and include a title at the top. Save as architecture.excalidraw in the current directory.
evals.expected_output: A valid .excalidraw JSON file with at least 6 rectangles, at least 5 arrows, and labeled text for each component.

assertions.name: file_exists
assertions.check: output file exists at expected path

assertions.name: valid_json
assertions.check: file parses as JSON

assertions.name: correct_top_level_type
assertions.check: type == 'excalidraw'

assertions.name: at_least_six_rectangles
assertions.check: at least 6 rectangles

assertions.name: at_least_five_arrows
assertions.check: at least 5 arrows (Client→Gateway, Gateway→{Auth,Users,Billing}, Users→DB, Billing→DB)

assertions.name: all_six_component_labels_present
assertions.check: labels for Client, Gateway, Auth, Users, Billing, Postgres

assertions.name: has_title
assertions.check: at least one text element with fontSize >= 28

assertions.name: all_required_element_fields_present
assertions.check: every element has all required schema fields

assertions.name: arrows_have_points_array
assertions.check: every arrow has points starting [0,0]

assertions.name: labels_centered_in_nodes
assertions.check: each component label's center falls inside a rectangle, roughly centered horizontally (within 25% of rect width)

assertions.name: arrows_connect_shape_edges
assertions.check: every arrow starts and ends within 20px of a rectangle's edge (arrow-routing failure mode)

assertions.name: arrows_avoid_crossing_nodes
assertions.check: no arrow passes through the interior of a rectangle that is not its endpoint

assertions.name: no_overlapping_nodes
assertions.check: no two rectangles' bounding boxes overlap

assertions.name: no_overlapping_text
assertions.check: no two text elements materially overlap

evals.name: annotated-screenshot
evals.prompt: Take ./test-assets/ref-1.png and create an Excalidraw file where the image sits in the center and there are exactly 4 red ellipse annotations placed at the four corners of the image (upper-left, upper-right, lower-left, lower-right). Each annotation should have a numbered label (1, 2, 3, 4) on the ellipse and a separate descriptive text label off to the side of the image with an arrow connecting the label back to its ellipse. The descriptions can be placeholder text (e.g., 'Upper-left region', 'Upper-right region', etc.). Save as annotated.excalidraw in the current directory.
evals.expected_output: A valid .excalidraw JSON file with 1 embedded image, exactly 4 ellipses, at least 4 descriptive text labels, and at least 4 arrows.

assertions.name: file_exists
assertions.check: output file exists

assertions.name: valid_json
assertions.check: parses as JSON

assertions.name: correct_top_level_type
assertions.check: type == 'excalidraw'

assertions.name: exactly_one_embedded_image
assertions.check: 1 file entry and 1 image element

assertions.name: exactly_four_ellipses
assertions.check: exactly 4 ellipses

assertions.name: ellipses_are_red
assertions.check: all 4 ellipses red

assertions.name: at_least_eight_text_elements
assertions.check: at least 8 text elements (4 numbers + 4 descriptions)

assertions.name: at_least_four_arrows
assertions.check: at least 4 arrows

assertions.name: numbered_labels_present
assertions.check: standalone '1','2','3','4' labels

assertions.name: all_required_element_fields_present
assertions.check: every element has required fields

assertions.name: ellipses_at_four_corners
assertions.check: ellipse centers fall one per quadrant of the image, near its corners

assertions.name: numbered_labels_on_ellipses
assertions.check: each numeral label's center lies on/within its ellipse

assertions.name: descriptions_outside_image
assertions.check: the 4 descriptive labels sit outside the image bounds

assertions.name: arrows_link_labels_to_ellipses
assertions.check: each arrow has one end near a descriptive label and the other near an ellipse (arrow-routing failure mode)

assertions.name: no_overlapping_text
assertions.check: no two text elements materially overlap
